name: the-great-influenza-the-story-of-the-deadliest-pandemic-in-history
description: John M. Barry's The Great Influenza: The Story of the Deadliest Pandemic in History — a medical history and pandemic preparedness toolkit chronicling the 1918 Spanish flu (50-100 million deaths), the scientific race to find the pathogen, the catastrophic political response under Wilson's wartime censorship, and the birth of modern medicine through public health disaster.
useCase: Understand the worst pandemic in history — the science, the political failures, the public health lessons, and the human tragedy that 50-100 million deaths left behind.
knownRisks: The book contains graphic descriptions of illness and death — may be distressing
Some scientific understanding has advanced since 2004 (e.g., reconstruction of the 1918 virus in 2005)
The political critique of Wilson is historically controversial and may feel one-sided
